q: What did students learn in Grade 11 regarding an object in circular motion?
a: Angular displacement, angular velocity, and angular acceleration.
Linear displacement, linear velocity, and linear acceleration.
Tangential velocity, tangential acceleration, and centripetal acceleration.
Rotational kinetic energy, torque, and angular momentum.

q: What concepts will be studied in this chapter for rotational motion?
a: Constant angular acceleration and centripetal acceleration.
Moment of inertia and rotational kinetic energy.

q: What is a key learning outcome for students in this chapter regarding types of motion?
a: Differentiate between circular motion and rotational motion.
Differentiate between translational motion and vibrational motion.
Differentiate between projectile motion and parabolic motion.
Differentiate between simple harmonic motion and wave motion.

q: Which aspect of rotational motion is expected for students to examine in this chapter?
a: Rotational motion under constant angular acceleration.
Rotational motion with variable angular acceleration.

q: What is another learning outcome related to quantities in this chapter?
a: Deduce the relations between angular and linear quantities.
Deduce the relations between energy and momentum.
Deduce the relations between force and work.
Deduce the relations between power and efficiency.

q: What specific type of acceleration is a learning outcome for students to understand?
What distinction are students expected to make regarding circular motion?
a: Distinguish between uniform circular motion and nonuniform circular motion.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces in circular motion.
Distinguish between open and closed circular paths.
Distinguish between internal and external forces in circular motion.

q: In circular motion, how does an object move?
a: The object just moves in a circle.
The object rotates about an axis within itself.
The object oscillates back and forth along a circular arc.
The object moves in a helical path.

q: What is an example of an object that moves in a circle at a constant height, as mentioned in the text?
a: Artificial satellites going around the earth.
The rotation of the Earth about its own axis.

q: In rotational motion, how does the object behave?
a: The object rotates about an axis.
The object moves in a straight line.
The object maintains a fixed orientation in space.

q: Which phenomenon is an example of rotational motion that creates the cycle of day and night?
a: Rotation of earth about its own axis.
Motion of the blades of a fan.

q: What defines a rigid body?
a: A body that does not deform or change shape.
A body that always moves in a straight line.
A body whose mass changes during motion.
A body that can easily be compressed.

q: What characteristic of a rigid body remains constant, regardless of how the body moves?
a: The distance between any two particles within the body.

q: When a rigid body is in rotational motion, what happens to all the particles constituting it?
a: They undergo circular motion about a common axis.
They move in parallel straight lines.
They move randomly without a common pattern.

q: What occurs when an object (rigid body) rotates and speeds up or slows down?
a: Its angular velocity is changing, leading to angular acceleration.

q: What condition must be met for angular acceleration to be considered constant?
a: If its angular velocity changes at a constant rate.
If its linear velocity changes at a constant rate.
If its position changes linearly with time.

q: What is the motion called when an object's angular acceleration is constant?
a: Rotational motion under constant angular acceleration.

q: Which variables are considered rotational variables that were previously introduced?
a: Angular displacement, angular velocity, and angular acceleration.
Linear displacement, linear velocity, and linear acceleration.

q: In this section, for what type of motion are rotational variables applied to analyze?
a: Rotational motion for a rigid body about a fixed axis under a constant angular acceleration.
Rotational motion of a deformable body.
Translational motion of a point mass.

q: What type of equations can be derived for an object that rotates with a constant angular acceleration?
a: Equations of motion for projectile trajectory.

q: Kinematic equations for rotational motion are expressed in terms of which quantities at any instant of time?
a: Angular displacement, angular velocity, and angular acceleration.
Linear displacement, linear velocity, and linear acceleration.
Torque, moment of inertia, and angular momentum.

q: If $\omega_0$ is the initial angular speed at $t = 0$, how is angular speed $\omega$ at time $t$ derived for constant angular acceleration?
What is the formula for average angular speed?
The angular displacement for a time interval $t$ is initially expressed as $\theta = \bar{\omega} t$. Which two equations are used to further express $\theta$ and $\omega$?
Which kinematic equation relates angular displacement, initial angular speed, angular acceleration, and time?
Which kinematic equation relates final angular speed, initial angular speed, angular acceleration, and angular displacement?
What is the relationship between the equations of motion for constant angular acceleration and those for linear motion?
a: They are the same, with angular quantities replaced by corresponding linear ones.
They are inverse of each other.
They are only applicable under specific, different conditions.

q: What is the linear counterpart of angular displacement ($\theta$)?
What is the linear counterpart of angular velocity ($\omega$)?
What is the linear counterpart of angular acceleration ($\alpha$)?
According to Table 1.1, what is the angular equivalent of the linear equation $v = v_0 + at$?
According to Table 1.1, what is the angular equivalent of the linear equation $s = v_0 t + \frac{1}{2} a t^2$?
According to Table 1.1, what is the angular equivalent of the linear equation $v^2 = v_0^2 + 2as$?
According to Table 1.1, what is the angular equivalent of the linear equation $\bar{v} = \frac{v + v_0}{2}$?
Under what condition is the angular acceleration of a rotating object equal to zero?
a: When there is no change in angular velocity.
When the angular velocity is increasing.
When the angular velocity is decreasing.
When the object is at rest.

q: The term "rotational motion" refers to an object's rotation about what?
a: A fixed point in space, without rotation.

q: "Angular displacement" is a key word referring to what?
a: The angle through which an object rotates.
The linear distance an object moves.
The rate of change of angular position.
The rate of change of angular velocity.

q: "Angular velocity" is defined as what?
a: The rate of change of angular displacement.
The rate of change of linear displacement.
The rate of change of angular acceleration.

q: "Angular acceleration" is defined as what?
a: The rate of change of angular velocity.
The rate of change of linear velocity.
The linear distance covered over time.

q: Which of the following describes circular motion?
a: An object just moves in a circle.
An object rotates about an axis.
An object moves in a straight line.
An object vibrates back and forth.
